title: Azure Functions
description: Serverless compute with a generous event-driven free grant for HTTP APIs, timers, and queue workloads.
content: Why it is worth using on free tier Azure Functions is one of the strongest free-tier options for teams that need API endpoints and event processing without paying for always-on infrastructure. The model works well when traffic is bursty and predictable. Operational notes - Keep function app startup weight low to reduce cold starts. - Put strict retry and dead-letter behavior around queue-triggered functions. - Pair it with Azure Storage and static frontends for low-cost full-stack projects.

title: Build a Free-Tier API + Docs Stack
description: Practical build path for shipping a fully static docs front-end with an event-driven API backend on free plans.
content: Outcome You will ship a static Next.js site on GitHub Pages and wire a minimal serverless API endpoint using free-tier limits as first-class constraints. Steps 1. Define the API boundary and expected request volume. 2. Choose a free-tier compute platform with predictable quotas. 3. Implement caching and retries early to reduce failed-request waste. 4. Export your docs frontend statically and deploy through CI. 5. Add budget and quota alerts before public launch. Validation checklist - Documentation builds are reproducible. - API failure modes are explicitly handled. - No single step depends on a paid-only feature.

title: Cloudflare Workers
description: Edge runtime with high global coverage and developer-friendly free request limits for APIs and lightweight middleware.
content: Why it is worth using on free tier Cloudflare Workers excels for globally distributed APIs and request shaping because the free tier is large enough for most hobby and early-stage production systems. Operational notes - Keep bundles small for faster deploy and startup. - Use KV and Durable Objects selectively; they can change performance profile. - Track per-route usage to avoid unexpected daily cap exhaustion.

title: Cloudflare Workers vs Azure Functions (Free Tier)
description: Comparison focused on global latency, quota behavior, and operational friction for small API workloads.
content: Decision framing Choose Cloudflare Workers when global low latency and edge routing are primary concerns. Choose Azure Functions when deeper Microsoft ecosystem integration and enterprise alignment matter more.

title: Free-Tier Quota Monitoring Starter
description: Quick reference for setting up quota watchpoints and failure alerts for free-tier stacks.
content: Use this resource as your baseline runbook section when launching on free tiers: - define hard limits and soft thresholds - document reset periods - set escalation policy before you need it

title: GitHub Actions
description: Workflow automation platform for CI/CD, content builds, and release pipelines with reusable workflow support.
content: Practical recommendation Use reusable workflows and pinned action versions from day one. That keeps the deployment pipeline auditable and easier to maintain as project scope grows.

title: GitHub Pages
description: Static hosting with custom domains and CI-based publishing that fits documentation and developer portals.
content: Why it is worth using on free tier GitHub Pages is a practical default when your project can be fully static. It removes backend complexity and pairs naturally with repository workflows. Operational notes - Keep output paths predictable for static hosting constraints. - Use repository-level environment variables to handle base path values. - Validate exported links after each deployment.

title: Launch a Cost-Neutral Developer Portal
description: Step-by-step execution plan for launching a searchable docs portal with free-tier hosting and CI.
content: Playbook phases 1. Model content schema and ingestion. 2. Build typed rendering and explorer UX. 3. Implement CI/CD for static deployment. 4. Add quota and release monitoring.
